pages.full_text:
been closed – Te Coop – for sale. We ate supper on the road outside (8.15) & then walked over to look at the magnificent view from the restaurant carpark (also Te Coop) So we moved the van & had the best view ever. By night, with the lights, the valley & hillside looked much more built-up than by day.
	Oh di! clicking in the night. The fridge has run out of gas, so I switch it off.

__Monday__
	Oh woe is me! For the first time in 23 years we have run out of gas – other tub empty too. (Norway doesn't count as we couldn't buy gas there). So no tea, coffee or toast & Simon shaved in cold water. We leave earlier than normal, 6 hours to get to Christina.
	We bought bread from a wood burning oven. The motorway after the nice Belgian fields was very boring. We had lunch soon after turning the corner near Cologne.
	At Christina's Anna Katarina met us at the gate. Christina & Victoria were also
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread; top of text is mid-sentence. 'Te Coop' appears to be a place name (possibly a restaurant/business name). 'Christina' is a personal name but 'Christina's' refers to a destination. 'Belgian fields' and 'near Cologne' suggest travel through Belgium into Germany. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; volume covers 2003-2019. 'Anna Katarina' appears at the bottom — personal name, not a place.

pages.full_text:
at home, but Michael & Eric[?12] & Constance were at Eric's school b.b.q. They arrived & we had a b.b.q supper in the beautiful garden. VW well-behaved children & Michael & Christina made us feel most welcome. The back is off the house. Michael is putting in insulation & will then replaster. Scaffolding. 2 of the 5 bedrooms are also under construction. We slept downstairs in the parents' bedroom. When the babies were little Michael looked after them until 2am & Christina [illegible] 2 am, unless all 3 cried at once. It must have been exhausting.
	On [?Wednesday] ~~Wednesday~~ Tuesday we went shopping with Christina for gas (& looked at camping vans, & food for a picnic. Michael took the afternoon off. Christina & I cycled to meet the children at school (who go to & fro on scooters). I saw the impressive after school club – a bistro for cooking, a sewing machine, a work bench. Christina said at first she was against
pages.notes: Top right corner: 'ate' is the continuation of the left page's last word (cut at gutter). A superscript '12' appears after 'Eric' — likely Eric's age, not a page number. The day heading reads 'Wednesday' with 'Tuesday' written above it as a correction (Wednesday crossed out, Tuesday is the intended reading). 'Constance' may also read 'Constancy' — best reading is 'Constance' as a proper name. Page number not visible in top right corner. Text continues onto next spread.
